title: Hlidskjalf Datagram
description: Event datagram for the Hlidskjalf messaging protocol. The envelope is self-describing: kind identifies what the datagram IS (content type), source identifies who SENT it (the emitting binary).

timestamp.description: Unix epoch timestamp in seconds (float64). When the datagram was created, not received.

source.description: The binary that emitted this datagram via datagram (e.g. "syn", "bifrost", "lockfile_monitor", "send_alert").

kind.description: Content type — what this datagram IS. Drives rendering (which component draws it) and filtering (which chip toggles it).

classifier.description: Sub-classification within the kind. Closed enum per kind, required for kinds with significant sub-types (traffic, quality). Absent for simple kinds.

priority.description: Severity level for threshold filtering and alert modality activation.

workspace.description: Project/workspace name (e.g. "yggdrasil", "bragi"). Empty string if not workspace-scoped.

detail.description: Human-readable one-line summary. Primary text for simple kinds (alert, canary, notify). May be absent when the payload speaks for itself.

speech.description: Voice alert text composed for spoken delivery. Triggers TTS when priority exceeds user's speech threshold.

payload.description: Structured data whose shape is determined by kind. quality: payload.quality.schema.json. traffic: payload.traffic.schema.json. Simple kinds (alert, canary, notify) have no payload.
